sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 1.2 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.1)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a group,$F$a free group with free generators$x_1, , x_n$, and$R$the free product of$G$and$F$. An equation$($in the unknowns$x_1, ,x_n)$over\/$G$is an expression of the form$v(x_1, \,x_n)=1$, where on the left is an element of$R$not conjugate in$R$to any element of$G$. We call$G$algebraically closed\/ if every equation over$G$has a solution in$G$. Do there exist algebraically closed groups...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
